title: dortmanna coronopifolia var. caerulea
intro: Dortmanna coronopifolia var. caerulea, also known as the blue water crowfoot, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Ranunculaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Dortmanna coronopifolia var. caerulea include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and act as a natural diuretic. The herbal preparation of this plant typically involves drying and infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to make a tea, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
